source_statement_summary: NGC 891 is seen exactly edge-on and shows a thick plane of dust and interstellar gas with filaments escaping into the halo.
High-resolution imaging reveals hundreds of extraplanar dust-absorbing structures visible to |z| < 1.5 kpc; associated high-z gas mass likely exceeds 2e8 solar masses.
NGC 891 is almost perfectly edge-on, has substantial H I halo, H-alpha envelope, thick-disc/halo structure, and small-scale halo substructure.
NGC 4217 is seen almost perfectly edge-on and is a candidate for studying extraplanar gas/dust structures above and below the plane.
The image shows dozens of dust structures, some reaching about 7000 light-years from the central plane, including columns, loops, and cones.
Radio continuum study of edge-on NGC 4217 targets extra-planar cosmic rays and magnetic fields; reports a large halo radio-bubble context.
